headline: Transcript: Ceasefire deal reached in Israel-Lebanon war
summary: Sonja Hutson talks to Malaika Kanaaneh Tapper and James Politi
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Ceasefire deal reached in Israel-Lebanon war’Sonja Hutson Good morning from the Financial Times. Today is Wednesday, November 27th. And this is your FT News Briefing. A ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is set to begin this morning. And the countries that Donald Trump wants to hit with tariffs are saying not so fast. Plus, Joe Biden is rushing to cement his legacy before he leaves office. I’m Sonja Hutson. And here’s the news you need to start your day. [MUSIC PLAYING]Israel’s security cabinet approved a ceasefire with Lebanon yesterday, and the caretaker government in Beirut is also expected to formally back the deal. This creates new hope that an end could be in sight for the year-long conflict between Israel’s forces and Hizbollah. The FT’s Malaika Tapper joins me now from Beirut to talk to us about these developments. Hi, Malaika.

Malaika Kanaaneh Tapper Hi there.

Sonja Hutson So we are speaking late Tuesday
